_doc: Hand-built example attack_paths[] entries for the OTSniffer demo plant fixture (data/plant-enriched.json). These illustrate the schema designed in scripts/otsniff/attack-paths.mjs and exercised by tests-js/attack-paths.test.mjs. Each entry is realistic — all protocols, leverage primitives, and MITRE ATT&CK for ICS T-codes are real, drawn from the energy-vertical kill-chain patterns in .claude/agents/blast-radius-pentester.md. The next implementer can render these directly in the UI without running any scanner. Source-finding IDs reference real entries in the demo plant's evidence_findings[]. path_confidence values were computed by computePathConfidence() and include cluster bonuses where applicable. Water-vertical examples are pending the next engagement.

attack_paths.rationale: Attacker on the engineering workstation reaches the SIS controller in 1 hop via the engineering protocol, downloads attacker-supplied safety logic, and silences the trip — TRITON/TRISIS pattern (Saudi Aramco 2017). Confidence elevated by TTP cluster overlap.

hops.leverage: TriStation engineering session over TSAA with cached project file; default keyswitch position on the safety controller

attack_paths.rationale: Attacker on the engineering workstation pivots to the boiler PLC via S7 over the OT VLAN, then issues unauthorized command messages to manipulate boiler setpoints — engineering-protocol abuse, no exploit needed.

hops.leverage: S7 engineering session; default operator-role credential on the controller still active (T0859 valid accounts)

attack_paths.rationale: Attacker dwelling on the Plant Historian uses cached PI Server credentials to reach the engineering workstation, then exploits an unauthenticated CIP service on the boiler PLC — Volt-Typhoon-class living-off-the-land via valid accounts and remote services.

hops.leverage: Cached PI Server service-account credential reused on the engineering workstation
Unauthenticated CIP service on the controller — verified per matched advisory ICSA-26-XXX-XX
